text: Lesson 6
zh: 第6课

text: Smash-and-grab
zh: 抢劫珠宝案

text: How did Mr. Taylor try to stop the thieves?
zh: 泰勒先生是如何试图阻止窃贼的？

text: The expensive shops in a famous arcade near Piccadilly were just opening.
zh: 皮卡迪利附近一条著名拱廊街上的高档商店刚刚开门营业。

text: At this time of the morning, the arcade was almost empty.
zh: 在早晨的这个时候，拱廊街几乎空无一人。

text: Mr. Taylor, the owner of a jewellery shop was admiring a new window display.
zh: 珠宝店老板泰勒先生正在欣赏新布置的橱窗。

text: Two of his assistants had been working busily since 8 o'clock and had only just finished.
zh: 他的两名店员从早上8点起就一直忙碌，刚刚完工。

text: Diamond necklaces and rings had been beautifully arranged on a background of black velvet.
zh: 钻石项链和戒指被精美地摆放在黑色天鹅绒的衬底上。

text: After gazing at the display for several minutes, Mr. Taylor went back into his shop.
zh: 泰勒先生凝视橱窗几分钟后，回到了店里。

text: The silence was suddenly broken when a large car, with its headlights on and its horn blaring, roared down the arcade.
zh: 突然，一辆开着大灯、按着喇叭的大车呼啸着冲进拱廊街，打破了寂静。

text: It came to a stop outside the jeweller's.
zh: 车子在珠宝店门外停了下来。

text: One man stayed at the wheel while two others with black stockings over their faces jumped out and smashed the window of the shop with iron bars.
zh: 一人留在方向盘前，另外两人用黑丝袜蒙面，跳下车用铁棍砸碎了商店的橱窗。

text: While this was going on, Mr. Taylor was upstairs.
zh: 这时，泰勒先生正在楼上。

text: He and his staff began throwing furniture out of the window.
zh: 他和店员们开始把家具从窗户往外扔。

text: Chairs and tables went flying into the arcade.
zh: 椅子和桌子飞向拱廊街。

text: One of the thieves was struck by a heavy statue,
zh: 一名窃贼被一尊沉重的雕像砸中，

text: but he was too busy helping himself to diamonds to notice any pain.
zh: 但他正忙着拿钻石，根本没顾上疼痛。

text: The raid was all over in three minutes, for the men scrambled back into the car and it moved off at a fantastic speed.
zh: 这次抢劫在三分钟内就结束了，因为那些人爬回车里，车子以惊人的速度开走了。

text: Just as it was leaving,
zh: 就在车子离开的那一刻，

text: Mr.Taylor rushed out and ran after it throwing ashtrays and vases,
zh: 泰勒先生冲出来追着车扔烟灰缸和花瓶，

text: but it was impossible to stop the thieves.
zh: 但根本无法阻止那些窃贼。

text: They had got away with thousands of pounds worth of diamonds.
zh: 他们带着价值数千英镑的钻石逃之夭夭。
